title: Building a Reliable Podcast Production Pipeline
summary: Episode 14, "Building a Reliable Podcast Production Pipeline" explores the production pipeline as the product: capture, post-production, content extraction, review/approval, and distribution. It emphasizes clean transcript vs raw transcript, standardized output bundles, consistent naming, and human review for names, links, sponsor language, and metadata.
description: A reliable podcast production pipeline is what turns a good conversation into a polished, publishable episode. Maya Chen joins the host to map the stages from capture and post-production through content extraction, review, and distribution. They also discuss why clean transcripts, consistent naming, verified links, and human approval matter more than heroic last-minute fixes.
show_notes: Episode 14: Building a Reliable Podcast Production Pipeline

In this episode, the host and Maya Chen break down why the production pipeline shapes the listener experience just as much as the recording itself. They cover five stages—capture, post-production, content extraction, review and approval, and distribution—and explain why a reliable workflow reduces errors downstream.
- Keep raw and cleaned transcripts separate.
- Standardize the output bundle for every episode.
- Use automation for drafts and formatting, but keep approval human.
- Verify names, links, sponsor language, and metadata before publish.
- Treat transcript content as a rich input, not the final source of truth.
Grounding note: release copy is based only on the clean transcript and the verified URL list.
